qa_pairs.question: Which `gromacs-plumed` module versions are available for CPU and GPU use in the `StdEnv/2023` and `StdEnv/2020` environments, and which PLUMED versions are integrated with them?
qa_pairs.answer: For the `StdEnv/2023` environment, `gromacs-plumed/2023.5` is available with PLUMED v2.9.2, and `gromacs-plumed/2020.7` with PLUMED v2.8.5. For the `StdEnv/2020` environment, available versions include `gromacs-plumed/2022.6` (PLUMED v2.8.3), `gromacs-plumed/2022.3` (PLUMED v2.8.1), `gromacs-plumed/2021.6` (PLUMED v2.7.4), `gromacs-plumed/2021.4` (PLUMED v2.7.3), `gromacs-plumed/2021.2` (PLUMED v2.7.1), and `gromacs-plumed/2019.6` (PLUMED v2.6.2). Each of these versions has distinct module paths for CPU and GPU usage, specifying the compiler, MPI, and CUDA versions as appropriate.

qa_pairs.question: How would one load the `gromacs-plumed/2020.7` module for running simulations on CPUs in the `StdEnv/2023` environment?
qa_pairs.answer: To load the `gromacs-plumed/2020.7` module for CPU simulations in the `StdEnv/2023` environment, you would use the command: `module load StdEnv/2023 gcc/12.3 openmpi/4.1.5 gromacs-plumed/2020.7`.

qa_pairs.question: Which standard software environments for `gromacs-plumed` modules are no longer supported?
qa_pairs.answer: The `StdEnv/2018` and `StdEnv/2016` standard software environments for `gromacs-plumed` modules are no longer supported, as indicated by the 'Deprecated' warning.
